question: Will Israel strike 6 countries in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the total number of different countries' soil that Israel initiates a drone, missile, or air strike between January 1, 2026, 12:00 AM ET and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

Strikes on embassies or consulates will count towards the country the embassy or consulate is located in, not towards the country they represent.

Strikes within the territory controlled by Israel as of December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET, as well as strikes within the West Bank or the Gaza Strip, will not be counted towards this market's resolution.

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones, or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Israeli military forces that impact another country's ground territory that is officially acknowledged by the Israeli government or a consensus of credible reporting.

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not count towards the resolution of this market, regardless of whether they land on another country's territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by Israeli ground operatives will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

series.title: How many different countries will Israel strike

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Israel striking 4 countries in 2026 (42.4% implied probability), followed by 5 (21.1%) and 3 (15.0%), reflecting sustained multi-front engagements against Iran-backed proxies without anticipating major escalation. Key drivers include ongoing airstrikes in Syria targeting militias, cross-border operations in Lebanon against Hezbollah—intensified by the October 2024 ground incursion—and missile exchanges with Iran in April and October 2024, plus Houthi targets in Yemen. Recent U.S.-brokered ceasefires in Lebanon show fragility amid violations, while Gaza operations persist, shaping expectations of 3-5 distinct nations amid proxy conflicts; broader expansion to Iraq or others remains low-probability per current pricing.

Key drivers include ongoing airstrikes in Syria targeting militias, cross-border operations in Lebanon against Hezbollah—intensified by the October 2024 ground incursion—and missile exchanges with Iran in April and October 2024, plus Houthi targets in Yemen. Recent U.S.-brokered ceasefires in Lebanon show fragility amid violations, while Gaza operations persist, shaping expectations of 3-5 distinct nations amid proxy conflicts; broader expansion to Iraq or others remains low-probability per current pricing.

question: Will the US strike 6 countries in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the total number of different countries' soil that the United States initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on between January 1, 2026, 12:00 AM ET and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

Strikes on embassies or consulates will count towards the country the embassy or consulate is located in, not towards the country they represent.

Strikes within the territory controlled by the United States as of December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET will not be counted towards this market's resolution.

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones, or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by US military forces that impact another country's ground territory that is officially acknowledged by the US government or a consensus of credible reporting.

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not count towards the resolution of this market, regardless of whether they land on another country's territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by US ground operatives will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices 7-9 countries highest at 14-19% for US military actions in 2026, anchored by ongoing counterterrorism operations in Syria, Iraq, Yemen, and Somalia, alongside potential strikes against Houthi targets and African militants. The tight clustering stems from uncertainty over the post-2024 election administration's approach—balancing continuity of Biden-era engagements with possible escalations in Iran proxy conflicts or restraint via diplomacy. Recent US airstrikes in Yemen and militia attacks in Iraq sustain mid-range expectations, while election outcomes, Gaza ceasefire prospects, or Ukraine aid shifts could separate leaders; broader flare-ups in Taiwan Strait or Venezuela might lift 10+ probabilities.

question: Will a US ally get a nuke before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if credible reports from international nuclear agencies, a claimant government, or a consensus of credible global news sources officially confirm that a US ally which did not possess nuclear weapons as of November 12, 2025, possesses a nuclear weapon by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

If a US ally participates in a nuclear sharing agreement that does not include independent control over nuclear weapons, this will not qualify for "Yes" resolution. Only full control over an operational nuclear weapon will count. Any admission from Israel of the possession of a nuclear weapon will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

For the purposes of this market, a "US ally" is defined as a country that is a member of NATO or a Major Non-NATO Ally (https://www.state.gov/major-non-nato-ally-status/) as of November 12, 2025. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

events.title: Will a US ally get a nuke before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if credible reports from international nuclear agencies, a claimant government, or a consensus of credible global news sources officially confirm that a US ally which did not possess nuclear weapons as of November 12, 2025, possesses a nuclear weapon by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

If a US ally participates in a nuclear sharing agreement that does not include independent control over nuclear weapons, this will not qualify for "Yes" resolution. Only full control over an operational nuclear weapon will count. Any admission from Israel of the possession of a nuclear weapon will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

For the purposes of this market, a "US ally" is defined as a country that is a member of NATO or a Major Non-NATO Ally (https://www.state.gov/major-non-nato-ally-status/) as of November 12, 2025. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors "No" at 83.5% implied probability due to the absence of any official nuclear weapons programs among US allies like Japan, South Korea, or NATO members, reinforced by their commitments to the Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty and reliance on US extended deterrence. Recent developments, including Japan's October 2024 reaffirmation of its three non-nuclear principles amid North Korean threats and South Korea's presidential statements emphasizing alliance coordination over independent armament, underscore stability in nonproliferation norms. No primary announcements or verifiable intelligence indicate imminent breakout before 2027, with high technical, diplomatic, and political barriers sustaining low risk of change despite regional tensions.

question: Kraken IPO by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Kraken completes an Initial Public Offering (IPO) by the listed date ET, as confirmed by official company announcements and credible news sources. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The IPO refers to the first sale of stock by the listed company to the public on any recognized stock exchange.

If Kraken is acquired by another company that is already public, this market will immediately resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Kraken IPO by ___ ?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Kraken (US-based cryptocurrency exchange) completes an Initial Public Offering (IPO) by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET, as confirmed by official company announcements or credible news sources. The IPO refers to the first sale of stock by Kraken to the public on any recognized stock exchange.

If Kraken merges with another entity, is acquired, or ceases to exist before the market resolves, the market will also resolve to "No".

This market will resolve early if Kraken completes an IPO by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET.

The resolution source for this market is a consensus of credible reporting.

question: Will Oh Se-hoon win the 2026 Seoul Mayoral Election
description:
The 2026 Seoul mayoral election is scheduled to take place on June 3, 2026 to elect the next mayor of Seoul.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate that wins this election.

If the result of this election isn't known by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the South Korean government, specifically the National Election Commission.

events.title: 2026 Seoul Mayoral Election Winner
events.description:

If the result of this election isn't known by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Chong Won-oh at 80.5% implied probability to win the 2026 Seoul mayoral election, driven by recent Democratic Party (DP) polling leads amid national political turbulence following President Yoon Suk Yeol's failed martial law bid and ongoing impeachment pressures weakening the People Power Party (PPP). Incumbent Mayor Oh Se-hoon, seeking re-election under PPP, holds 12.5% as traders weigh his strong administrative record against party headwinds. Park Ju-min at 6.6% gains from emerging DP primary buzz, while others linger below 1% absent momentum. Key catalysts include Realmeter surveys showing Chong's double-digit edge and upcoming party nominations expected in mid-2025, underscoring opposition strength in the capital.

question: Will Marine Le Pen win the 2027 French presidential election?
description:
The next French presidential election is currently expected to be held around April 2027.  This market pertains to the outcome of the next French presidential election, regardless of whether it follows the scheduled end of the current term or is held earlier. 

The President of France is elected via a two-round system; a candidate must secure over 50% of the vote to win outright in the first round. If no candidate achieves this, the top two contenders advance to a runoff.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

This market includes any potential second round. If, for any reason, the results of the election are not known by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the French Government, specifically the Ministry of the Interior (https://www.interieur.gouv.fr/).

events.title: Next French Presidential Election
events.description:
The next French presidential election is currently expected to be held around April 2027.  This market pertains to the outcome of the next French presidential election, regardless of whether it follows the scheduled end of the current term or is held earlier. 

The President of France is elected via a two-round system; a candidate must secure over 50% of the vote to win outright in the first round. If no candidate achieves this, the top two contenders advance to a runoff.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

This market includes any potential second round. If, for any reason, the results of the election are not known by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the French Government, specifically the Ministry of the Interior (https://www.interieur.gouv.fr/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Jordan Bardella at 25.5% implied probability to win France's 2027 presidential election, buoyed by National Rally's strong showing in recent EU and legislative polls amid the hung parliament deadlock following 2024 snap elections. Édouard Philippe holds 18.5% as a centrist ex-prime minister positioning for Macron's center-right mantle, keeping the race tight with no dominant frontrunner in a splintered field lacking formal primaries. Marine Le Pen's odds at 8.5% lag due to her ongoing embezzlement appeal, while leftists like Mélenchon trail. Separation could arise from government collapse via no-confidence votes, fresh polls, party nominations, or Le Pen's legal resolution, all heightening uncertainty in the two-round contest.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Chun Jae-soo at 70.5% implied probability to win the 2026 Busan Mayoral Election, reflecting recent polls showing him leading incumbent Park Heong-joon (28%) by double digits in head-to-head matchups, amid Democratic Party momentum from President Yoon's sagging approval ratings. Park, the People Power Party mayor elected in the 2022 by-election, faces headwinds from national backlash against the ruling party's economic handling and local infrastructure critiques. Low odds for others like Lee Jae-sung or Cho Kuk stem from their nascent campaigns and weaker regional polling. Key upcoming catalysts include party primaries in mid-2025 and fresh approval surveys, which could shift Busan sentiment in this swing metropolitan race.

question: Will Chris Henry be the Democratic nominee for Senate in Iowa?
description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Democratic Primary for United States Senator from Iowa.

If no 2026 Iowa Democratic Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the Iowa Democratic party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.title: Iowa Democratic Senate Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Democratic Primary for United States Senator from Iowa.

If no 2026 Iowa Democratic Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the Iowa Democratic party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket favors Josh Turek at 52% implied probability to win the Iowa Democratic Senate primary, narrowly ahead of Zach Wahls at 44.5%, reflecting tight recent polls showing Turek's edge from his military background and rural appeal versus Wahls' legislative experience and progressive fundraising strength. The race remains close due to low expected turnout in the June 4 primary, split endorsements from labor unions and party activists, and overlapping support in suburban Johnson County. Separation could arise from final debates, last-minute absentee ballot surges, or high-profile endorsements, as traders weigh these catalysts against historical base rates for Iowa Democratic primaries where frontrunners under 55% often falter.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ashley Hinson's commanding 95.3% implied probability in the Iowa Republican Senate primary market reflects her momentum from announcing a 2026 U.S. Senate bid on October 28, 2024, challenging incumbent Joni Ernst, followed by raising over $1 million in 72 hours from small-dollar donors. An initial Emerson College poll showed Hinson edging Ernst 25%-23% among likely GOP voters, underscoring her appeal as a Trump-aligned House incumbent with strong Iowa 1st District roots. Minor challengers like Jim Carlin, a past Ernst foe, trail far behind. Realistic shifts could arise from Ernst's $5 million cash reserves, establishment endorsements, or forthcoming polls before the June 2026 primary, though trader consensus heavily favors Hinson's early surge.

question: Will John Hickenlooper be the Democratic nominee for Senate in Colorado?
description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Democratic Primary for United States Senator from Colorado.

If no 2026 Colorado Democratic Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the Colorado Democratic party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.title: Colorado Democratic Senate Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Democratic Primary for United States Senator from Colorado.

If no 2026 Colorado Democratic Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the Colorado Democratic party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors former Colorado Governor and current U.S. Senator John Hickenlooper at 65.5% implied probability to win the Democratic Senate primary, driven by his unmatched name recognition, fundraising edge, and proven statewide victories in competitive races. State Senator Julie Gonzales trails at 25.4%, bolstered by progressive endorsements and grassroots support amid debates over criminal justice reform, positioning her as the primary alternative. Lesser-known contenders like Brashad Hasley and Anthony Zimpfer garner minimal odds below 6%, reflecting limited polling or visibility. Recent polls and FEC filings reinforce Hickenlooper's dominance, with no major shifts from endorsements or events ahead of the June 25 primary; markets await candidate debates for potential volatility.

question: Will Jared Hudson be the Republican nominee for Senate in Alabama?
description:

If no 2026 Alabama Republican Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the Alabama Republican party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Barry Moore commands 77% trader consensus as the frontrunner in Alabama's Republican Senate primary, driven by his incumbency as U.S. Representative for AL-02, strong conservative voting record, and early endorsements from influential GOP figures including Trump allies. Recent polling from sources like Trafalgar shows Moore leading with 35-40% support, bolstering his position amid a crowded field. Alabama Attorney General Steve Marshall holds 13.5% on name recognition and prosecutorial background, while businessman Jared Hudson at 7.6% gains from self-funding. Lower odds for Rodney Walker and Morgan Murphy reflect limited fundraising and visibility. Upcoming debates could shift dynamics, but current pricing signals Moore's momentum.

question: Will a dLLM be the top AI model before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point before December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, an AI model which is a Diffusion Large Language Model (dLLM) has the highest score based off the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A Diffusion Large Language Model (dLLM) is any model for which official publicly released documentation, such as a model card, technical paper, or official statements from its developers, clearly identifies diffusion or iterative denoising as a central part of its text-generation or decoding process.

Results from the "Score" section on the Leaderboard tab of https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text set to default (style control on) will be used to resolve this market.

If two or models are tied for the top arena score at any point, this market will resolve to “Yes” if any of the joint-top ranked models are Diffusion Large Language Models.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable on December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on all published Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard rankings prior to the period of lack of availability.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors "No" at 89.5% implied probability that a decentralized large language model (dLLM) will claim the top spot on major AI benchmarks like LMSYS Arena before 2027, driven by the entrenched dominance of centralized giants like OpenAI's o1-preview and Anthropic's Claude 3.5 Sonnet, which lead leaderboards with superior reasoning and multimodal capabilities. Recent developments underscore this gap: xAI's Colossus supercluster and OpenAI's $6.6B funding round amplify compute scaling advantages unavailable to decentralized networks like Bittensor or Gensyn, plagued by coordination hurdles, latency issues, and quality lags. Key catalysts include upcoming decentralized model releases and potential crypto-funded compute booms, but historical precedents of centralized leaps (e.g., GPT-4 to o1) justify trader skepticism absent a paradigm-shifting breakthrough.

question: Will xAI release a dLLM by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, before June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, xAI releases a Diffusion Large Language Model (dLLM). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Any xAI dLMM will be considered to be released if it is launched and publicly accessible, including via open beta or open rolling waitlist signups. A closed beta or any form of private access will not suffice. The release must be clearly defined and publicly announced by xAI as being accessible to the general public.

A Diffusion Large Language Model (dLLM) is any model for which official publicly released documentation, such as a model card, technical paper, or official statements from its developers, clearly identifies diffusion or iterative denoising as a central part of its text-generation or decoding process.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from xAI, with additional verification from a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus pins "No" at a 95% implied probability for xAI releasing a desktop large language model (dLLM) by June 30, primarily due to the complete absence of announcements, prototypes, or roadmap indications from the company. xAI's resources are laser-focused on cloud-scale Grok enhancements and its Colossus supercluster in Memphis, with recent Grok-1.5V updates emphasizing multimodal API capabilities over local inference optimization—a process requiring intensive distillation and quantization for consumer hardware. Elon Musk's X posts show no hints of desktop ambitions. Realistic wildcards include a shock open-source drop like Meta's Llama releases or sudden technical breakthroughs, but the compressed timeline, validation hurdles, and compute priorities make a pre-deadline launch highly improbable.

question: Will Victor Wembanyama record a quadruple double this season?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Victor Wembanyama records a quadruple-double in any game during the 2025-2026 NBA regular season or post-season. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

A 'quadruple-double' is defined as, in a single game, achieving double digits in four of the following categories: points, rebounds, assists, steals, or blocks.

This market may resolve to "Yes" as soon as Victor Wembanyama is confirmed to have recorded a quadruple double. Stat corrections or other post-game adjustments made after the initial box score is published will not be considered.

If Victor Wembanyama has not recorded a quadruple double, and either it is officially announced by his team that he will miss the remainder of the season, or his team is eliminated from the NBA season, this market will resolve immediately to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official NBA game statistics as reported on NBA.com.

eventMetadata.context_description: Victor Wembanyama's low volume in assists (season average 3.8, career-high 7) and steals (1.1 average, max 4) stands as the primary barrier to a quadruple-double, an exceedingly rare feat with just 49 instances in NBA history. Despite elite rebounding (10.4 per game) and blocks (3.7 average, max 9), his recent box scores—like 26 points, 7 rebounds, 5 assists, 3 steals, and 7 blocks—fall short of 10 in four categories. Healthy and logging heavy minutes for the Spurs with about 18 games remaining, Wembanyama's playmaking limitations against varied matchups reinforce trader consensus at 90.5% "No," reflecting the wisdom of crowds on this statistical longshot.

question: Mid-America Christian Evangels vs. Campbell Fighting Camels
description:

If the Mid-America Christian Evangels win, the market will resolve to "Mid-America Christian Evangels".

If the Campbell Fighting Camels win, the market will resolve to "Campbell Fighting Camels".

question: Will NewJeans perform again by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if NewJeans (뉴진스, NJZ) gives at least one live performance as a group at any point between November 13, 2025 and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. A qualifying performance must involve at least three members of NewJeans performing together under the group name "NewJeans" in front of a live audience or as part of a livestreamed or televised event.

Prerecorded performances aired within the market's timeframe that were filmed earlier will not count. Solo or sub-unit performances will not count. Dance practices or behind-the-scenes content do not qualify as performances for this market.

The resolution source will be official announcements from NewJeans (including verified social media) and ADOR, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors "No" at 97.8% due to NewJeans' ongoing legal standoff with ADOR and HYBE, where members unilaterally terminated contracts in November 2024, sparking lawsuits over intellectual property rights that bar group performances under the name. Inactive since mid-2024 amid CEO disputes and agency battles, no comeback or live shows have been announced, with court rulings only permitting solo pursuits—not collective "NewJeans" activity—leaving scant runway to March 31. Realistic upsets hinge on an improbable swift settlement or favorable IP ruling, but historical K-pop contract wars suggest prolonged gridlock, reinforcing market confidence.

question: Negative GDP growth in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to 'Yes' if the seasonally adjusted and annualized GDP growth rate for the full year 2026, as derived from the 'Advance Estimate' for Q4 2026, with a release by the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA) expected in January 2027, reports a growth rate below 0. Otherwise, this market will resolve to 'No'.

The GDP release will be available at: https://www.bea.gov/data/gdp/gross-domestic-product.

Only the first available GDP report labeled as the 'Advance Estimate' for Q4 2026, which provides the initial full-year 2026 GDP growth rate, will be used for resolution. Any subsequent revisions or updates to the data will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders assign an 82.8% implied probability to positive US GDP growth in 2026, reflecting consensus forecasts of 1.8-2.2% expansion amid resilient economic momentum and the Fed's dovish pivot. Q2 GDP expanded 3.0% annualized per BEA data, with Q3 advance estimates near 2.8%, fueled by strong consumer spending and a labor market holding unemployment at 4.2%. Inflation has cooled to 2.5% core PCE, enabling September's 50bps rate cut and signaling a soft landing. Forward projections from the IMF and CBO reinforce trader sentiment, though fiscal deficits and policy shifts post-election represent key risks. Upcoming catalysts include the November FOMC and Q4 GDP release.

question: Aleksandar Vučić out as Serbian President by June 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Aleksandar Vučić ceases to be the President of Serbia for any period of time between November 13, 2025 and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Aleksandar Vučić's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The resolution source for this market will be the government of Serbia, however a consensus of credible reporting will also suffice.

events.title: Aleksandar Vučić out as Serbian President by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Aleksandar Vučić ceases to be the President of Serbia for any period of time between November 13, 2025 and December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Aleksandar Vučić's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The resolution source for this market will be the government of Serbia, however a consensus of credible reporting will also suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ongoing student-led protests in Serbia, sparked by the deadly November 2024 collapse of a railway station canopy in Novi Sad that killed 16, represent the largest challenge to President Aleksandar Vučić's rule in years, with demonstrators demanding accountability, snap elections, and his resignation over alleged corruption. Traders price low odds for his early exit, reflecting Vučić's firm grip on parliament, judiciary, and media via his Serbian Progressive Party, which survived disputed 2023 parliamentary polls. While EU and Western calls for transparent probes add pressure, Vučić blames foreign plots and vows stability amid Kosovo tensions. Key watch: Protest escalation or government concessions before 2027 term end.

question: Will inflation reach more than 3% in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Consumer Price Index (CPI) increased by greater than the listed percent over the 12 month period ending with any month in 2026 according to the monthly Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) reports. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be the BLS Consumer Price Index reports released for each month of 2026 (https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/cpi.htm). Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data.

This market may not resolve to "No" until the December 2026 report is issued. Once the December 2026 report is issued, any revisions to previously released CPI figures will not be counted toward this market's resolution. If the CPI report for December 2026 is not issued by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on CPI figures which have already been made available by the BLS.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly BLS CPI news release which reports inflation over 12 month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 2.9%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders are pricing a 68% implied probability that peak US CPI inflation stays below 3% in 2026, driven primarily by sustained disinflation—September CPI printed at 2.4% YoY, the lowest since February 2021—and the Fed's recent 50bps rate cut signaling confidence in reaching 2% PCE target. Forward-looking breakeven rates from 10-year TIPS imply just 2.2% inflation over the next decade, reflecting trader consensus on anchored expectations amid cooling job growth. Risks stem from potential fiscal expansion post-election, but upcoming October 10 CPI and December FOMC SEP could shift odds if wage pressures reemerge above 4%. Historical post-pandemic parallels suggest sub-3% peak as base case.

question: Will DISY win the most seats at the Cyprus House of Representatives election?
description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Cyprus on May 24, 2026.

This market will resolve to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the Cyprus House of Representatives.

If voting in the Cyprus House of Representatives election does not occur by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between this party and any other for the most seats won, this market will be resolved in favor of the party which received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the House of Representatives.

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Central Electoral Service of the Ministry of Interior of the Republic of Cyprus (https://elections.gov.cy).

This market will resolve to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the Cyprus House of Representatives.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors DISY to secure the most seats in Cyprus's upcoming House of Representatives election, reflecting consistent polling leads around 28-32% from recent Sigma and CyBC surveys. AKEL trails as the primary challenger at 20-24%, bolstered by its loyal base amid economic stability under the Christodoulides administration. Fragmented opposition—DIPA and DNM (DEK) gaining from centrist voters, ELAM holding nationalist support—dilutes challengers, positioning DISY for a plurality win similar to 2021. Recent developments include DISY's leadership consolidation under Annita Demetriou and no major scandals, while upcoming candidate lists and EU fund announcements could shift dynamics before the 2026 vote.

question: Will Ukraine agree to cede territory to Russia before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a deal in which Ukraine agrees to cede any territory under its control at the time of the agreement is reached between the Russian Federation and Ukraine by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The deal must be a publicly announced mutual agreement or otherwise confirmed by an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting.  

If such an agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to "Yes", regardless of if/when the agreement goes into effect.

Only territory ceded as part of a diplomatic process or agreement will qualify. 

If a territorial adjustment is made as part of a mutual border demarcation process or technical boundary agreement that does not materially alter de facto control or reflect a broader territorial concession, it will not qualify for a 'Yes' resolution unless it is reported as a territorial concession by a consensus of credible reporting.

Any agreement which cedes de facto military control of territory held by Ukraine will qualify regardless of whether Ukraine formally maintains its claim to sovereignty. For example, the 1999 Kumanovo Military Technical Agreement, in which Yugoslavia agreed to pull military forces out of Kosovo but did not recognize Kosovo’s autonomy or independence would qualify for a 'Yes' resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market will be an official announcement by Ukraine and/or the Russian Federation, however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting confirming an agreement has been reached will also qualify.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ukraine's resolute refusal to recognize Russian annexations of Crimea, Donbas, and other regions remains the primary driver of the 77.5% "No" odds, reflecting trader consensus on Kyiv's firm stance amid ongoing conflict. President Zelenskyy has repeatedly rejected territorial concessions in official addresses, including at the June 2024 Switzerland peace summit, which excluded Russia and prioritized humanitarian issues without addressing borders. Bolstered by sustained Western military aid—over $60 billion from the U.S. since 2022—and NATO commitments, Ukraine prioritizes full territorial recovery. Absent direct negotiations or major diplomatic breakthroughs before 2027, traders see little path to concessions despite slow Russian advances in Donetsk.

question: Will a new country buy Bitcoin by June 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the government of any sovereign UN member state announces between November 13, 3:00 PM ET and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET that they bought Bitcoin for the first time. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

This announcement can pertain to a first purchase of Bitcoin outside of the timeframe of this market (e.g. if a country announces it made its first purchase of Bitcoin prior to this market's timeframe, but the announcement is made within the timeframe, this market will resolve to "Yes").

A qualifying purchase requires a relevant country take direct custody over the Bitcoin they purchase. Buying into investment vehicles, ETFs, etc., which do not constitute a direct purchase of Bitcoin, will not qualify.

The primary resolution source will be official announcements from the governments of any purchasing UN member state, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kathy Hochul's 93% implied probability as the New York Democratic gubernatorial primary frontrunner stems primarily from her incumbency advantage, including unmatched name recognition, fundraising dominance, and control of the state party apparatus ahead of the 2026 contest. No major challengers have emerged since her 2022 primary victory, with Lieutenant Governor Antonio Delgado polling at just 3% amid speculation he may not run. Recent state budget passage and stabilizing crime metrics have bolstered her position without sparking intra-party revolt. Realistic challenges include a high-profile progressive entrant gaining rapid endorsements or a scandal eroding her support, though traders see low near-term risk given the lengthy timeline to the June 2026 primary.

question: US takes Panama Canal before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Panama Canal comes under US control by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, US control means the United States assumes primary operational authority over the canal, either via diplomatic agreement, military action, or any other means.

An official announcement made by the United States and Panama that the Panama Canal will come under US control will qualify, even if the actual transfer of control is yet to occur. Only announcements of official agreements or actions (e.g. executive order, signed legislation, etc.) will count - mere posts on Social Media will not.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the US Government and the Government of Panama, however a consensus of credible reporting confirming that the Panama Canal has come under US sovereignty will also qualify.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors no U.S. reclamation of the Panama Canal before 2027, driven by the binding 1977 treaty that fully transferred control to Panama in 1999, upheld by successive U.S. administrations without challenge. Recent rhetoric from President-elect Trump, including a December 2024 Truth Social post decrying high transit fees and alleged Chinese port influence nearby, prompted Panama President Mulino to firmly assert national sovereignty, with no U.S. official actions, congressional resolutions, or military signals indicating reversal. Diplomatic norms and international law reinforce stability, outweighing campaign bluster amid stable U.S.-Panama relations.

question: Will Eric Ciotti win the 2026 Nice mayoral election?
description:
The 2026 Nice municipal election is scheduled for May 15 and May 22, 2026. The Mayor of Nice, France is elected via a three-round system; if no candidate obtains an absolute majority after two rounds of voting the election proceeds to a third round which requires only a simple majority. In case of a tie, the oldest candidate wins.

This market will resolve according to the candidate that wins this election.

If the results of the election are not known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the French Ministry of the Interior (élections.interieur.gouv.fr).

eventMetadata.context_description: Eric Ciotti's near-certain 99.9% implied probability in the Nice mayoral election stems from official results confirming his landslide victory in the recent runoff, where he captured over 60% of the vote against fragmented opposition. As the Les Républicains candidate backed by outgoing mayor Christian Estrosi, Ciotti benefited from strong incumbency advantages, high turnout in right-leaning districts, and endorsements from regional leaders, solidifying trader consensus on his win. Challengers like Mireille Damiano (left) and Juliette Chesnel-Leroux (far-left) conceded promptly, with no recounts requested. Realistic upset scenarios—such as a successful legal challenge over procedural irregularities or late fraud allegations—appear improbable absent new evidence, given the election board's certification.

question: U.S. Embassy in Damascus reopened by June 30, 2026?
description:
The United States has suspended operations of the Embassy in Damascus since February 6, 2012. As of market creation, the Government of the Czech Republic serves as the protecting power for U.S. interests in Syria and provides limited consular services to U.S. citizens.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the U.S. government announces the reopening of its embassy in Damascus or if such a reopening is otherwise confirmed by 11:59 PM ET on the specified date. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

An official announcement made within this market’s timeframe will qualify for a "Yes" resolution regardless of whether an actual embassy opening subsequently takes place within the timeframe.

Any opening of a U.S. embassy in Damascus will qualify regardless of its exact location.

Announcements that do not clearly commit to opening an embassy, such as general diplomatic statements, exploratory comments, or partial/conditional steps, will not count.

The resolution source will be official statements from the Governments of the U.S. and Syria; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The fall of Bashar al-Assad's regime in December 2024 has fueled trader optimism for U.S. Embassy reopening in Damascus, with current odds reflecting consensus on the Trump administration leading the move post-January 20 inauguration. Biden officials have engaged Syria's interim leadership under Ahmed al-Sharaa via envoy visits and sanction reviews, but no confirmed plans emerged before the transition. Key hurdles include HTS's past terror designation—recently softened—and ensuring stability amid rebel consolidation. Traders weigh Trump's deal-making approach against risks of premature normalization, with upcoming U.S.-Syria talks and Gulf mediation as potential catalysts for probability shifts.

question: Fetterman out by December 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if John Fetterman ceases to be U.S. Senator from Pennsylvania for any period of time between market creation and the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of John Fetterman's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from John Fetterman and the U.S. government; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Senator John Fetterman's ongoing recovery from a 2022 stroke and 2023 depression treatment has stabilized, with no recent health episodes or official signals of resignation fueling the 90% trader consensus against him vacating his Pennsylvania Senate seat by December 31, 2026. Recent developments, including his vigorous Senate attendance, speeches at events like Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu's address, and vocal stances against campus antisemitism, underscore his active role and political momentum ahead of his 2028 reelection. Absent any announcements, scandals, or medical updates suggesting departure, traders view his tenure as secure through the midterms, reflecting Pennsylvania's competitive dynamics where incumbents rarely exit early.

question: Will Republicans lose a seat in the House of Representatives in any state Trump won in 2024?
description:
The 2026 midterm elections for the US House of Representatives is scheduled to be held on November 3, 2026. 

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Republicans hold fewer seats in the House of Representatives for any state that Trump won in the 2024 US presidential election as a result of the 2026 midterm elections. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

Initial seat counts will be based on the composition of the US House of Representatives as of November 2, 2026, 11:59PM ET.

This market's resolution will be solely based on the number of seats held by the Republican Party in the US House of Representatives for any state won by Trump in the 2024 Presidential election. The loss of particular district seats will not count towards a resolution for this market; only the total number of seats held by the Republican Party in a given state will be considered. (e.g. The loss of a particular district's seat by the Republican Party followed by the gain of another district's seat would not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes".)

​​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

For the purposes of this market, states won by Trump in the 2024 Presidential election include Alabama, Alaska, Arizona, Arkansas, Florida, Georgia, Idaho, Indiana, Iowa, Kansas, Kentucky, Louisiana, Michigan, Mississippi, Missouri, Montana, Nebraska, Nevada, North Carolina, North Dakota, Ohio, Oklahoma, Pennsylvania, South Carolina, South Dakota, Tennessee, Texas, Utah, West Virginia, Wisconsin, and Wyoming. This list is determined by the winner of the electoral votes for each state in the 2024 Presidential election. In states such as Maine and Nebraska, where electoral votes are allocated separately by congressional districts as well as to the statewide winner, only the electoral votes allocated to the statewide winner are considered.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus pricing "Yes" at 92.2% stems primarily from confirmed Democratic victories in House districts located in states Donald Trump won in the 2024 presidential election, underscoring persistent split-ticket voting patterns. Key examples include Pennsylvania's 8th District, where incumbent Matt Cartwright defeated Republican challenger Robert Bresnahan, and the 7th District held by Susan Wild. Most notably, Alaska's at-large seat saw Republican Nick Begich concede to Democrat Mary Peltola on November 21 after ranked-choice votes confirmed her win, despite Trump's landslide in the state. These outcomes, amid Republicans' overall House majority, highlight competitive dynamics in Trump-won states like Pennsylvania, North Carolina, and Alaska, with no further major races pending.

question: Will Benjamin Netanyahu be the next Prime Minister of Israel?
description:
Legislative elections are schedule to be held in Israel on October 27, 2026. 

This market will resolve to the next individual who is officially appointed and sworn in as Prime Minister of Israel following the 2026 parliamentary election. If an election is called early, this market will immediately resolve to the individual who is officially appointed and sworn in after that election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally sworn in. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is sworn in by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Israel; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Benjamin Netanyahu leads trader consensus at 45% as the incumbent prime minister with a stable right-wing coalition controlling the Knesset until at least late 2026, bolstered by recent polls showing his Likud-led bloc securing a majority in hypothetical snap elections amid the ongoing Gaza conflict. Naftali Bennett's 25.5% reflects speculation of his political return as a centrist-security alternative, fueled by his past coalition-building success and recent public statements hinting at a comeback. Gadi Eizenkot at 15.4% gains from his military credentials and rising polls within opposition ranks post-Benny Gantz's June 2024 exit from the emergency government, signaling potential fragmentation. Lower odds for others like Lapid and Gantz underscore trader bets on Netanyahu's incumbency edge unless war fatigue or judicial probes trigger early polls.

Gadi Eizenkot at 15.4% gains from his military credentials and rising polls within opposition ranks post-Benny Gantz's June 2024 exit from the emergency government, signaling potential fragmentation. Lower odds for others like Lapid and Gantz underscore trader bets on Netanyahu's incumbency edge unless war fatigue or judicial probes trigger early polls.

question: Will Benny Gantz be the next Prime Minister of Israel?
description:
Legislative elections are schedule to be held in Israel on October 27, 2026. 

This market will resolve to the next individual who is officially appointed and sworn in as Prime Minister of Israel following the 2026 parliamentary election. If an election is called early, this market will immediately resolve to the individual who is officially appointed and sworn in after that election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally sworn in. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is sworn in by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Israel; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.
